SnowDistribution: Wet snowpack.  No visibility throughout the day above 600 metres.  Winds 30-40 mph, progress difficult and tiring.
Comment: Mountain conditions are likely to be poor. Old snow becoming very icy again.
WeatherInfluences: Rain will turn to snow as the freezing level drops, light snow fall expected throughout the day above 800 metres. Gale force winds throughout, South-South-West around dawn, veering to the West in the afternoon.
SnowStability: Accumulations of fresh unstable windslab will develop mainly on North through East to South-East aspects above 800 metres. Steep wind sheltered locations such as coire rims and gully tops will be most affected particularly where accumulations develop over old snow. Cornice stability will remain poor. Old snow ice will become very firm and icy. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A wet day with the freezing level rising above the summits in the afternoon. Strong winds from the South.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is wet and soft becoming firmer with altitude. The snow cover remains generally well bonded. Cover is still reasonable above 850 metres on West through North to East aspects. Stability of remaining cornices are suspect. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Limited patchy snow in specific places. Cloud base was at 700 metres all day. Winds 20 -30 mph progress difficult at times with stronger gusts
WeatherInfluences: Wet and mild overnight then cooler from midnight with persistent showers of snow above 900m, later 800m. Very strong South-West, later West-South-West winds.
SnowStability: The old moist snowpack will refreeze overnight. New weakly bonded windslab will form and begin to gain depth on steep North to East aspects above 900m. Weak cornice development anticipated above steepest lee slopes of the same aspects. Good stability in all other locations. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Cold and dry overnight then milder during the day. Precipitation during the day was briefly wintry over summits during the morning, turning to occasional light rain showers later. Strong South West winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The patchy snowpack is moist and firm at all altitudes and well stabilised throughout. Largest expanses of old snow lie on North West through North to North East aspects above 800m but lower in some favoured gullies. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Diminishing patchy snow cover.  Cloud base was around 700 metres most of the day. Winds 30 -40 mph.
Comment: Colder temperatures with some snow forecast for Thursday and Friday.
WeatherInfluences: Overnight will be mild with gale force South-Westerly winds and rain at all levels. Rain turning to snow above around 800 metres in the morning then easing to light showers for the remainder of the day. Winds will veer West-South-Westerly and ease slightly during the day.
SnowStability: The existing patchy snow cover will remain well bonded and stable. The snow surface will become increasingly firm as the freezing level falls. Isolated windslab deposits possible on some steep North-East to South-East aspects above around 850 metres on the more inland hills in our area. These deposits are expected to be shallow and easily avoidable. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A mild day with Strong South-Westerly winds. Drizzle on the summits for most of the day with some periods of heavier rain.
ObservedSnowStability: The remaining patchy snow cover continues to thaw and diminish. Stability is good in all locations. Largest areas remain on North-West through North to East aspects above 750 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Wet but firm snow throughout. Poor visibility. Winds 30 -40 mph.
Comment: Existing snowpack will become very firm and icy at higher levels.
WeatherInfluences: The freezing level will drop  to around 900 metres overnight, and continue down to 800 metres by the end of the day. Gale force winds will be from the South-West. There will be some snow showers overnight and during the day.
SnowStability: The existing snowpack will freeze and consolidate. Fresh deposits of windslab will accumulate in sheltered locations on mainly North to East aspects above 1000 metres. However, these deposits are not expected to be extensive or deep. The tops of gullies and coire rims will be most affected. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: The freezing level dropped to around 900 metres overnight before rising to above the summits during the day. It was a cloudy day with some light rain at times.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack became firm and icy overnight. During the day it started to soften. Stability was generally good. However, some substantial cornices remain above 1100 metres, and and these were starting to slump and show signs of instability in the mild conditions. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Firm snow despite the rain,  becoming moist later. Cloudy on the tops and in the high coires. Winds 20-30 mph on the summits.
Comment: The snow surface is expected to be firm and consequential in the event of an unattested fall or slide.
WeatherInfluences: Precipitation is expected throughout the period. This will fall as rain at all elevations during Tuesday night before transitioning to snow showers above 700 metres during the day. Winds will be mostly South-Westerly, veering to the West-South-West and West during the day. Storm force overnight, gale to very strong during the day. The freezing level will be 1700 metres overnight falling to 800 metres during day light hours.
SnowStability: Unstable windslab will accumulate as the day progresses on North-West through North to East aspects above 900 metres. Accumulations are expected to be isolated and avoidable, confined to gully tops and coire headwalls. The existing snowpack will become firm and icy above 700 metres with a dusting of new snow from this level upwards.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Precipitation fell as rain at most levels with a trace of wet snow above 1000 metres. Winds were very strong and from the South. At midday the freezing level was around 1100 metes.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack was firm and stable at all elevations above 950 metres, with a trace of wet snow at summit level. The largest continuous areas of snow are found on North-West through North to East aspects above 870 metres. Ribbons and isolated patches are found below this altitude. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: The snowpack continues to soften during the day.  Cloud base was at 800 metres most of the all day.  Sunny spells below this height.  Winds 30 - 40 mph in exposed areas, increasing during the day.
Comment: Rapid thaw conditions this evening before a return to colder and more seasonal weather producing a variety of avalanche problems over the next 24 hours.
WeatherInfluences: After overnight heavy rain, fresh snow will fall to around 700 metres from midnight onwards. Storm force Southerly winds during the night will veer to the West-South-West easing slightly through the day. The high freezing level will lower to around 800 metres from midnight onwards.
SnowStability: At first, wet snow instabilities will be present and any existing cornices will be prone to collapse. Later, fresh unstable windslab will gradually build on North through East to South-East aspects above 800 meters in steep wind sheltered locations. Fragile cornices will start to develop on these same aspects. Localised areas of instabilities will develop below this height but will not be extensive. The old snowpack will be generally stable throughout. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Very strong South-South-Westerly winds through the day with the freezing level around 1200 metres at midday.  Generally a dry day but thick cloud producing some drizzle.
ObservedSnowStability: Snowpack stability is generally good in all locations, softening through the day. In deep accumulations some localised instabilities are persisting on very steep North and North-East aspects above 1000 metres but these are diminishing and are not extensive. Lingering cornices continue to be a hazard. The avalanche hazard is Low.
